title: The Slow Hope of Forest Conservation
subtitle: 森林保护：缓慢的希望

paragraphs.en: In the long view, the human relationship with forests has been one of brutal destruction, but even it carries elements of slow hope. In the Middle Ages, there was no shortage of timber in most parts of the world, and few saw cutting down forests as a problem. Yet in 1548 the people of Venice estimated that an important timber supply would last only 30 years at their current rate of usage — but different forest management would make it possible to meet the demand for many centuries to come.
paragraphs.zh: 从长远来看，人类与森林的关系一直是一种残酷的破坏，但即便如此也蕴含着缓慢的希望元素。在中世纪，世界大部分地区都不缺木材，很少有人将砍伐森林视为问题。然而1548年，威尼斯人估计按他们当时的使用速度，一项重要的木材供应只能再维持30年——但不同的森林管理方式将使其有可能满足未来许多世纪的需求。

paragraphs.en: The idea of preserving resources came out of a concern for the future: a fear of using up resources faster than they could be replenished. Economic interests were at the core of this understanding of trees and forests. It would take more than three centuries before scientists began to understand that timber production is not the only, and possibly not the most important, function of forests.
paragraphs.zh: 保护资源的理念源于对未来的担忧：担心资源消耗速度快于补充速度。经济利益是这种对树木和森林理解的核心。科学家们花了三个多世纪才开始认识到，木材生产并非森林的唯一功能，也可能并非最重要的功能。

paragraphs.en: The late 19th and early 20th century saw an increasing recognition that forests serve as habitats for countless animal and plant species that all rely on each other. They take over protective functions against soil erosion and landslides; they make a significant contribution to the water balance as they prevent surface runoff; they filter dirt particles, greenhouse gases and radioactive substances from the air; they produce oxygen; they provide spaces for recreation and they preserve historic and prehistoric remains. As a result, forests around the world have been set aside as parks or wilderness areas.
paragraphs.zh: 19世纪末和20世纪初，人们越来越认识到森林是无数相互依存的动植物物种的栖息地。它们承担起防止水土流失和山体滑坡的保护功能；通过防止地表径流对水平衡做出重大贡献；过滤空气中的尘粒、温室气体和放射性物质；产生氧气；提供休闲空间并保存历史和史前遗迹。因此，世界各地的森林被划为公园或荒野保护区。

paragraphs.en: Recent years have seen a big change in our view of forests. Peter Wohlleben's book The Hidden Life of Trees (2015), an international bestseller, suggests that trees can warn each other of danger through a 'wood wide web' of roots and fungi. They support each other through sharing of nutrients and information, and they even keep ancient stumps alive by feeding them solutions of sugars. Such insights have made us aware of deep ecological relationships between humans and the more-than-human world.
paragraphs.zh: 近年来我们对森林的看法发生了巨大变化。彼得·沃莱本的《树木的隐秘生活》（2015年）是国际畅销书，书中提出树木能通过由根系和真菌构成的“森林万维网”相互警示危险。它们通过分享养分和信息相互支持，甚至通过向古老树桩输送糖溶液来维持其生命。这些见解让我们意识到人类与超越人类的世界之间深刻的生态关系。

paragraphs.en: Awareness of ecologies is a recent phenomenon. It was not until the 1940s that the concept of the 'environment' embracing all living and nonliving things developed. In the 1970s, the term 'environment' gained currency, becoming widely adopted in the English and Romance languages, and as 'Umwelt' ('surrounding world') in German. The emergence of the idea led to the rise of environmental agencies, regulations and environmental studies, and to environmental science as new, integrated academic disciplines. It was in 1956 that the very first bachelor of science in environmental studies was awarded, at the State University of New York College of Forestry at Syracuse. Since the 1970s — with the rise of 'environmentalism' — environmental studies programmes have sprung up at hundreds of universities.
paragraphs.zh: 生态意识是最近才出现的现象。直到20世纪40年代，涵盖所有生物和非生物的“环境”概念才得以发展。20世纪70年代，“环境”一词开始流行，在英语和罗曼语中被广泛采用，德语中称为“Umwelt”（“周围世界”）。这一概念的出现催生了环境机构、法规和环境研究的兴起，并使环境科学成为新的综合性学科。1956年，纽约州立大学锡拉丘兹森林学院颁发了首个环境研究理学学士学位。自20世纪70年代“环保主义”兴起以来，数百所大学纷纷开设了环境研究课程。

paragraphs.en: There is slow hope in the fact that scholars from many different disciplines have adopted the term 'environment' over the past decades. They are exploring intricate connections within and between complex ecologies, as well as the impact that human environment-making (through techno-industrial, economic and other manipulative developments) has had on the biosphere. The rise of the idea of the environment and a scholarly understanding of ecological processes has influenced new technologies and also politics.
paragraphs.zh: 缓慢的希望在于，过去几十年来许多不同学科的学者都采用了“环境”一词。他们正在探索复杂生态内部及之间的错综联系，以及人类通过技术工业、经济及其他操控性发展所进行的环境改造对生物圈产生的影响。环境观念的兴起和对生态过程的学术理解已经影响了新技术和政治。

paragraphs.en: We have come to ask questions about vulnerability and risk, world ecologies, and the relationship between nature and power. The search for an adequate response to climate change occupies centre stage in international diplomacy. Social and environmental activists, scientists and indigenous groups have called the Paris Agreement of the United Nations Climate Change Conference in December 2015 insufficient, weak, or compromised. To some extent, they are right: climate change has already destroyed tens of thousands of livelihoods, and the situation will worsen in the near future for millions of mostly poorer people, who will join the ranks of those who have already been displaced by climate change and extreme weather events.
paragraphs.zh: 我们开始追问脆弱性与风险、世界生态以及自然与权力之间的关系。寻求应对气候变化的适当对策已成为国际外交的中心议题。社会与环境活动家、科学家和原住民团体称2015年12月联合国气候变化大会达成的《巴黎协定》不够充分、软弱或打了折扣。在某种程度上他们是对的：气候变化已摧毁了数以万计的生计，而在不久的将来，数百万以贫困人口为主的民众处境将更加恶化，加入因气候变化和极端天气事件而流离失所者的行列。

paragraphs.en: But the Paris Conference nevertheless marked a historic step toward the recognition of the need for action on climate change, the cutting of carbon emissions, and world cooperation. There were 195 nations that came to the table in Paris and agreed to limits on emissions. Historically, nothing comparable had happened prior to this. Before the 20th century, a handful of scientists had been interested in the theoretical relationship between greenhouse gases and climate change, but only the empirical evidence accumulated since the late 20th century established a clear connection between the burning of fossil fuels and a vastly accelerated rise in global temperatures.
paragraphs.zh: 但巴黎会议仍然标志着人们认识到需要就气候变化采取行动、减少碳排放和开展全球合作的历史性一步。有195个国家来到巴黎谈判桌前并同意限制排放。从历史角度看，此前从未发生过类似事件。在20世纪之前，只有少数科学家对温室气体与气候变化之间的理论关系感兴趣，但直到20世纪末积累的经验证据才在化石燃料燃烧与全球气温急剧加速上升之间建立了明确的联系。

words.meaning: adj. 经验主义的，以实验为依据的

paragraphs.en: The current crisis is not the first that humans have encountered, and a look at the struggles with pollution in recent history reveals transformations that once seemed unimaginable. The 'London fog' that came to define the capital through British novels and thrillers is in reality smog or smoke, a legacy of industrialisation. After a century of ignorance, London was hit by the Great Smog of December 1952 — the worst air-pollution event in the history of the United Kingdom which caused the deaths of approximately 12,000 people. Shortly thereafter, public initiatives and political campaigns led to strict regulations and new laws, including the Clean Air Act (1956). Today, London has effectively reduced traffic emissions through the introduction of a Congestion Charge Zone in 2003, and an Ultra Low Emission Zone in 2019.
paragraphs.zh: 当前的危机并非人类遭遇的第一次，回顾近代史上与污染的斗争，可以看到曾经看似难以想象的转变。通过英国小说和惊悚片定义伦敦的“伦敦雾”实际上是烟雾，是工业化的遗产。经过一个世纪的无视，伦敦遭遇了1952年12月的“大烟雾”——英国历史上最严重的空气污染事件，导致约12000人死亡。此后不久，公众倡议和政治运动催生了严格的法规和新法律，包括1956年的《清洁空气法》。如今，伦敦通过2003年引入交通拥堵费和2019年引入超低排放区，有效减少了交通排放。

paragraphs.en: Scientific evidence that we are living in an era of climate change, resource exhaustion and potential ecological disaster is overwhelming. How do we motivate a public exhausted by never-ending scenarios of doom and disaster, when the challenges seem so huge and so impossible to solve? Statistics about extinction and the gloom of decline will not in themselves get us out of our often self-created ecological traps: instead, they are more likely to result in paralysis and inaction.
paragraphs.zh: 我们生活在一个气候变化、资源枯竭和潜在生态灾难的时代，这一科学证据是压倒性的。当挑战看起来如此巨大且无法解决时，我们如何激励一个被无休止的末日场景搞得筋疲力尽的公众？关于灭绝的统计数据和衰退的阴霾本身并不能让我们摆脱常常是自己造成的生态陷阱：相反，它们更可能导致瘫痪和不作为。

paragraphs.en: We need stories and histories of change and transformation: ecological stories that make us confront the fact that human power is potentially destructive, and that the survival of our species on this planet depends on the preservation of soil and water, and the habitats and ecological systems. It is time that we showed successes and accelerations in ecological awareness, action and restoration: stories that include past successes and future visions about the rise of urban gardening and of renaturalised riverscapes, of successful protests against polluted air and water, of the rise of regional markets and slow food, and the planting of trees around the globe, of initiatives and enterprises that work towards ecological restoration.
paragraphs.zh: 我们需要关于变革与转型的故事和历史：那些让我们直面人类力量具有潜在破坏性、而我们物种在这颗星球上的生存取决于土壤、水源、栖息地和生态系统保护这一事实的生态故事。是时候展示生态意识、行动和恢复方面的成功与加速了：这些故事应包括过去的成功和未来的愿景，涉及都市园艺和恢复自然的河流景观的兴起、针对污染空气和水的成功抗议、区域市场和慢食的兴起、全球范围的植树活动，以及致力于生态恢复的倡议和企业。

paragraphs.en: The reality of ecological curses seems far greater than the power of the hopes left at the bottom of Pandora's box. But if we believe that nothing can be changed, then we are giving up our opportunity to act. Today's saving powers will not come from a deus ex machina. In an ever-more complex and synthetic world, our saving powers won't come from a single source, and certainly not from a too-big-to-fail approach or from those who have been drawn into the whirlpool of our age of speed.
paragraphs.zh: 生态诅咒的现实似乎远远大于留在潘多拉盒底的希望之力。但如果我们相信什么都无法改变，那就是放弃了行动的机会。今天的拯救之力不会来自“机械降神”。在一个日益复杂和合成的世界里，我们的拯救之力不会来自单一来源，当然也不会来自“大到不能倒”的方式，或那些被卷入速度时代漩涡的人。

words.meaning: n. 解围之神，意外救星

paragraphs.en: Hope can work as a wakeup call. It acknowledges setbacks. The concept of slow hope suggests that we can't expect things to change overnight. If the ever-faster exhaustion of natural resources (in ecological terms) and the 'shrinking of the present' (in social terms) are urgent problems of humans, then cutting down on exhaustive practices and working towards a 'stretching of the present' will be ways to move forward.
paragraphs.zh: 希望可以起到警醒的作用。它承认挫折的存在。缓慢希望的概念表明，我们不能期待事物在一夜之间改变。如果自然资源日益加速的枯竭（从生态角度看）和“当下的收缩”（从社会角度看）是人类的紧迫问题，那么减少过度消耗的行为并努力实现“当下的延伸”将是前进的道路。
